title: Waldheimia vestita
taxonomy: Waldheimia vestita is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It is native to the Himalayan region. The plant is classified within the genus Waldheimia, which consists of a small group of species found in the Himalayas and surrounding areas. Waldheimia vestita is a perennial herb with small white flowers and is often found growing in moist, shaded areas. The plant is often used in traditional medicine in the regions where it is native.
